sources.title: Direct parse of references 42-48 and legacy runtime consumers
facts: References 42-48 contain 1,142 non-empty lines, 8 Markdown tables with 79 data rows, and 145 H2/H3 sections.
Food therapy, source grades, historical formulas and modern safety annotations are interleaved; complete preservation does not make any entry food- or product-eligible.

sources.title: Rules for substances traditionally used as both food and Chinese medicinal materials
facts: Food-medicine substances must have traditional food use, be listed in the Chinese Pharmacopoeia, and pass directory and safety review.
Labels and marketing may not claim health functions or disease prevention and treatment.

sources.title: NHC 2025 response on the current food-medicine directory
facts: The current directory contains 106 substances and remains dynamically managed.
Directory inclusion and safety limits are material-specific; a historical medicine-food label is not automatic current inclusion.

sources.title: Chinese citizen health literacy explanations
facts: Health foods are foods, not medicines, and cannot replace medicines for disease treatment.
Medical symptoms and emergencies require appropriate professional care rather than food or supplement substitution.

sources.title: Healthy diet
facts: Adequacy, balance, moderation and diversity are the four core principles of a healthy diet.
A healthy diet must also be safe and free from microbial and chemical contaminants.

sources.title: Dietary and Herbal Supplements
facts: Herbal supplements can interact with medicines and pose condition- or procedure-specific risks.
Safety and effectiveness require product- and use-specific evidence.

sources.title: FDA dietary supplements and disease claims
facts: Herbs and dietary supplements are not FDA-approved to treat or prevent disease.
Food or supplement status does not establish a disease-treatment indication.

sources.title: Poisoning first aid
facts: Do not induce vomiting or use a cure-all antidote unless directed by poison control or a health professional.
Suspected poisoning needs immediate professional guidance.

sources.title: Signs and Symptoms of Stroke
facts: Sudden neurologic deficits require emergency response and immediate medical care.
Food, tea, or traditional formula advice must not delay evaluation.

sources.title: Treating Tuberculosis
facts: Tuberculosis requires a complete prescribed antimicrobial regimen.
Food therapy and historical remedies are not substitutes for treatment.

sources.title: Types of Cancer Treatment
facts: Cancer treatment depends on cancer type and stage and may include surgery, medicines, radiation and other evidence-based care.
Historical food or formula claims do not establish cancer treatment efficacy.

boundaries: These comparators establish food eligibility, healthy-diet, supplement safety, poisoning, emergency and disease-treatment boundaries; they do not validate individual historical recipes.
No A/B grade, food-therapy, medicine-food, tea, juice, porridge, dose, disease mapping or home action in references 42-48 becomes runtime eligible in this layer.
Current directory membership, edible part, processing, limit, population and disease evidence require item-level adjudication.
